title: Active Effort Key to Community Building
summary: The article argues that communities require active effort to build and maintain, contrasting passive consumption with the need for grassroots initiative and vulnerability. This perspective is significant because it challenges the common passive attitude toward social scenes, highlighting that social alienation may stem from a lack of personal investment in community fabric. The piece draws on personal experience and generational observations, noting that grassroots social institutions like clubs and dances have declined, and questions why these were not passed down.
context: Community building is often misunderstood as something that happens naturally, but it actually requires intentional effort and vulnerability. The article connects this to broader social trends where people see themselves as passive consumers rather than active participants.
discussion: Commenters share personal experiences of the vulnerability required to be the &\#x27;social fabric,&\#x27; noting that it can lead to toxic inner dialogs when effort is not reciprocated. Others observe a generational rift where older social institutions were not passed down to younger generations.
